description: Upwork RSS monitor: poll an Upwork saved-search RSS feed on a schedule, deduplicate against a Data Table of seen job links, and email Gmail notifications for new projects only.

conversation.text: Monitor Upwork RSS feed for new projects, read project details, and notify me via email

Plan: Schedule Trigger (every 15 minutes) → RSS Feed Read (Upwork saved-search feed) → loop each job → Data Table lookup by job link → if unseen, record the job and send a Gmail notification with title, snippet, and link; if already seen, skip. Which Upwork RSS URL and recipient email should I wire up, and do you prefer Gmail or another sender?

text: For every HITL step during the build — keep the run moving without stalling. Allow all permissions. Use any of the existing credentials or skip.
Use Gmail and check every 15 minutes. Skip the RSS URL and recipient email for now — leave placeholders I can fill in later.

conversation.text: I'll wire Schedule Trigger (every 15 min) → Read Upwork RSS → loop jobs → Data Table lookup → new-job check → record seen job + Gmail alert for new items only, with placeholders for the feed URL and recipient email. Ready for me to build it?

outcomeExpectations: The workflow is triggered by a Schedule Trigger node (n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger) configured to poll on a recurring interval (every 15 minutes in the source thread).
An RSS Feed Read node (n8n-nodes-base.rssFeedRead) fetches Upwork project entries from an RSS feed URL.
A Data Table is used to track previously seen Upwork job links so the same project is not notified twice.
The workflow loops over RSS items (e.g. Split In Batches or equivalent) and checks each job link against the Data Table before alerting.
A decision node (If, Filter, or equivalent) branches on whether the job link is new / not yet recorded.
When a job is new, the workflow inserts or upserts a row in the Data Table and sends a Gmail notification with project details (title, description/snippet, and link).
When a job was already seen, the workflow skips the email notification for that item.

executionScenarios.name: new-upwork-project
executionScenarios.description: RSS feed contains a project that has not been seen before
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The Schedule Trigger fires. RSS Feed Read returns one Upwork project item with title 'Build an n8n automation', link 'https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~0123456789', contentSnippet about the project, and isoDate '2026-06-29T07:00:00.000Z'. The Data Table lookup for that job_link returns no matching row. The new-job decision node routes to the unseen branch. The Data Table insert returns success. The Gmail send node returns success.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors on the new-project path only: Schedule Trigger → RSS Feed Read → loop item → Data Table lookup → new-job branch → Data Table insert + Gmail notification. A row is written to the Data Table recording the job link/title. A Gmail message is sent about the new Upwork project, including the title and a link to view it. Nodes on the already-seen/skip branch are not executed for this item.

executionScenarios.name: already-seen-upwork-project
executionScenarios.description: RSS feed contains a project whose link is already in the Data Table
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The Schedule Trigger fires. RSS Feed Read returns one Upwork project item with link 'https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~0123456789'. The Data Table lookup returns an existing row for that job_link (same link already recorded from a prior run). The new-job decision node routes to the already-seen branch. The loop continues without sending email for this item.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors on the skip path only: Schedule Trigger → RSS Feed Read → loop item → Data Table lookup → already-seen branch. No Gmail notification is sent for the duplicate project. The Data Table insert/record node on the new-project branch is not executed for this item.
